title: ophrys apifera subsp. purpurea
intro: Ophrys apifera subsp. purpurea, also known as the bee orchid, is a perennial orchid native to Europe and North Africa. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, which have been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Ophrys apifera subsp. purpurea include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
